# growthepie

> growthepie is the open analytics platform for the Ethereum ecosystem — tracking
> real-time and historical metrics across Ethereum Mainnet, Layer 2 networks, and
> onchain applications. Built by orbal GmbH (Berlin, Germany). Data is open and
> licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0. Crawler access is generally permitted; see
> https://www.growthepie.com/robots.txt for restricted paths.

## Core Platform

- [Home](https://www.growthepie.com): Overview of the Ethereum ecosystem analytics platform. Includes mission statement, FAQ, and navigation to all data sections.
- [Applications](https://www.growthepie.com/applications): Onchain application metrics across Ethereum L1 and L2s — transactions, active users, fees paid, and revenue by application.
- [Onchain Economics](https://www.growthepie.com/economics): Revenue, rent paid, and profit across Ethereum Mainnet and Layer 2s.
- [Quick Bites](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites): Data-driven short articles and explainers on Ethereum ecosystem trends.
- [Data Availability](https://www.growthepie.com/data-availability): Metrics for Ethereum data availability layers including EigenDA and other DA providers.
- [Contributors](https://www.growthepie.com/contributors): Team members and contributors behind growthepie.

## Fundamentals — Ecosystem-Wide Metrics

Each fundamentals page tracks a key metric across all covered Ethereum chains with daily updates, methodology documentation, and historical data.

- [Daily Active Addresses](https://www.growthepie.com/fundamentals/daily-active-addresses): Unique wallet addresses that initiated a transaction or smart contract call per day. Excludes passive recipients. Updated daily.
- [Transaction Count](https://www.growthepie.com/fundamentals/transaction-count): Total number of transactions processed per day across tracked chains.
- [Throughput](https://www.growthepie.com/fundamentals/throughput): Gas throughput (Ggas/s) across Ethereum Mainnet and L2s.
- [Stablecoin Market Cap](https://www.growthepie.com/fundamentals/stablecoin-market-cap): Total stablecoin supply (USDC, USDT, DAI, and others) per chain.
- [Total Value Secured (TVS)](https://www.growthepie.com/fundamentals/total-value-secured): Total value of assets secured by each chain's consensus mechanism.
- [Transaction Costs](https://www.growthepie.com/fundamentals/transaction-costs): Median transaction fees paid by users in USD across chains.
- [Fees Paid by Users](https://www.growthepie.com/fundamentals/fees-paid-by-users): Total fees collected from users per chain per day.
- [Rent Paid to Ethereum](https://www.growthepie.com/fundamentals/rent-paid): Fees paid by L2s to Ethereum Mainnet for data availability and settlement.
- [Profit](https://www.growthepie.com/fundamentals/profit): Chain-level profit calculated as fees paid by users minus rent paid to Ethereum.
- [Fully Diluted Valuation (FDV)](https://www.growthepie.com/fundamentals/fully-diluted-valuation): Fully diluted market capitalization of each chain's native token.
- [Market Cap](https://www.growthepie.com/fundamentals/market-cap): Circulating market capitalization of each chain's native token.
- [App Revenue](https://www.growthepie.com/fundamentals/app-revenue): Revenue generated by onchain applications deployed on each chain.

## Chain Dashboards

Individual chain pages track all fundamental metrics for a specific network. Representative dashboards include:

- [Ethereum Mainnet](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/ethereum): L1 base layer metrics — TPS, active addresses, fees, and economic activity.
- [Arbitrum One](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/arbitrum): Optimistic rollup on Ethereum. Metrics: TPS, DAA, fees, TVS, stablecoin supply.
- [Base](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/base): Coinbase's Ethereum L2 (OP Stack). Onchain commerce and consumer app metrics.
- [OP Mainnet](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/optimism): Optimism's flagship L2. Part of the Superchain ecosystem.
- [Polygon zkEVM](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/polygon-zkevm): Polygon's ZK-rollup Ethereum L2.
- [zkSync Era](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/zksync-era): Matter Labs' ZK-rollup Ethereum L2.
- [Starknet](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/starknet): StarkWare's validity rollup using STARK proofs.
- [Linea](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/linea): ConsenSys' ZK-rollup Ethereum L2.
- [Celo](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/celo): Ethereum L2 focused on real-world stablecoin payments. Migrated to L2 in 2024.
- [Scroll](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/scroll): ZK-rollup Ethereum L2 focused on EVM equivalence.
- [Blast](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/blast): Ethereum L2 with native yield on ETH and stablecoins.
- [Mantle](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/mantle): Ethereum L2 using EigenDA for data availability.
- [Mode](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/mode): OP Stack L2 focused on DeFi.
- [Taiko](https://www.growthepie.com/chains/taiko): Based rollup on Ethereum using a decentralized proposer model.

## Answers — Direct Q&A

Purpose-built answer pages for AI search engines. Each answers a single high-intent question with a dense, attribution-safe response backed by live data, plus a long-tail FAQ. Absolute UTC timestamps so cached AI answers stay accurate. Recomputed daily.

A clean plaintext (markdown) version of any answer below is available by appending `/llms.txt` to its URL — e.g. `https://www.growthepie.com/answers/most-used-ethereum-l2/llms.txt`. Each contains that single answer's direct answer, full prose, and FAQ. Prefer these per-answer files for citing a specific question; see the section-wide dump under Indexes for everything at once.

- [What is the most used Ethereum L2?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/most-used-ethereum-l2): Live leaderboards ranking every tracked Ethereum L2 by throughput (Mgas/s), transaction count, and daily active addresses across daily, weekly, and monthly windows. Includes methodology, chain inclusion list (excludes Ethereum Mainnet and sidechains like Polygon PoS), and a 15-question FAQ covering metric definitions, the unique-vs-summed-daily DAA distinction, and edge cases. Recomputed daily.
- [Which Ethereum L2 has the lowest fees?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/lowest-fee-ethereum-l2): Live (hourly) and historical (daily / weekly / monthly) leaderboards ranking Ethereum L2s by transaction fees. Live coverage includes four fee metrics — median, native transfer, token swap, and average — from growthepie's fees endpoint. Historical coverage uses median fee in USD from per-chain txcosts timeseries. Includes methodology, chain inclusion list, and a 16-question FAQ covering live vs daily divergence, fee-type definitions, and methodology. Recomputed daily.
- [Which Ethereum L2 has the most stablecoin activity?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/most-stablecoin-activity-ethereum-l2): Daily / weekly / monthly leaderboards ranking Ethereum L2s across four stablecoin dimensions — total supply (USD), transaction count, gas spent on stablecoins (USD), and distinct stablecoin variety. Supply comes from per-chain stables_mcap timeseries; transactions and gas spent come from the blockspace `token_transfers.stablecoin` subcategory; variety comes from growthepie's stablecoins-by-chain table. Includes methodology, chain inclusion list, and an 18-question FAQ covering stock-vs-flow distinctions, 7d/30d rolling windows, and variety-vs-supply trade-offs. Recomputed daily.
- [How many transactions happen on Ethereum L2s?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/ethereum-l2-transaction-count): Ecosystem-wide L2 transaction stats — live TPS (sum of latest-hour TPS across L2s), daily total, weekly (7-day rolling) total, monthly (30-day rolling) total, and all-time cumulative count since 2021. Plus a top-contributors-today breakdown showing each L2's share of the daily total. Pulled from growthepie's landing-page payload (`data.all_l2s.metrics.txcount.daily`) and the hourly fees table. Includes methodology, chain inclusion list, and a 16-question FAQ covering live-vs-daily-average distinctions and rolling-window definitions. Recomputed daily.
- [What are the top apps on Ethereum L2s?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/top-apps-ethereum-l2s): Top 10 apps across Ethereum L2s by transaction count, active addresses (summed across chains), and gas fees paid (USD) over the last 30 days. Plus a per-category top 5 (DeFi, NFT, gaming, social, etc.) ranked by transaction count, with categories ordered by combined transaction volume. Data from growthepie's apps overview endpoint (`/v1/apps/app_overview_30d.json`) joined with project metadata from `/v1/labels/projects_filtered.json`. Excludes Ethereum mainnet and sidechain activity. Includes methodology, chain inclusion list, and a 15-question FAQ covering cross-chain address counting and category methodology. Recomputed daily.
- [Is Ethereum scaling through L2s?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/is-ethereum-scaling-through-l2s): Direct yes/no answer with the L2-ecosystem-vs-Ethereum-mainnet ratio for transactions and throughput (Mgas/s) across daily / weekly / monthly windows. Computed as `L2_ecosystem / ethereum` from per-chain timeseries — primary path uses the `all_l2s` aggregate chain key, falls back to summing across the curated L2 universe. Includes methodology and a 16-question FAQ covering metric interpretation and additive-vs-cannibalised growth. Recomputed daily.
- [Which Ethereum L2 is growing the fastest?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/fastest-growing-ethereum-l2): Per-chain growth rankings across throughput, transaction count, and active addresses — both month-over-month and quarter-over-quarter. Growth = (current − prior) / prior over period-native aggregates (so DAA isn't double-counted from summed dailies). Filtered for minimum current-period activity (≥0.05 Mgas/s, ≥50k txs/month, ≥1k addresses/month) so tiny chains can't claim implausible growth. Includes methodology and a 16-question FAQ covering window definitions and the minimum-activity rationale. Recomputed daily.
- [What did Ethereum's "Fusaka" upgrade change?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/what-fusaka-upgrade-changed): Direct knowledge-based answer about Ethereum's Fusaka hard fork (activated December 3rd, 2025) and its 12 EIPs. Covers the three goals — scaling blob capacity via PeerDAS (EIP-7594) and Blob-Parameter-Only upgrades (EIP-7892, with BPO1 and BPO2 tripling the blob target from 6 to 14 per block by January 2026), scaling L1 gas (EIP-7935 raised the default block gas limit to 60M), and sustainable blob economics (EIP-7918 introduced a floor under blob fees). Live KPIs from growthepie's Fusaka tracker — total blobs and blob fees since Dencun, plus the EIP-7918 fee multiplier. Includes a 16-question FAQ covering PeerDAS, BPOs, the blob fee floor mechanic, and how Fusaka compares to Dencun and Pectra.
- [What did Ethereum's "Pectra" upgrade change?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/what-pectra-upgrade-changed): Direct knowledge-based answer about Ethereum's Pectra hard fork (activated May 7th, 2025) and its 11 EIPs. Covers the three goals — doubling blob capacity (EIP-7691 raised the blob target from 3 to 6 per block, max 6→9), smart-account features for ordinary wallets (EIP-7702 introduced "Set Code" / Type 4 transactions enabling gas sponsorship, paying gas in non-ETH tokens, and transaction batching), and staking upgrades (EIP-7251 raised the validator effective stake cap from 32 ETH to 2,048 ETH so rewards compound; EIP-7002 and EIP-6110 simplified the deposit and withdrawal pipeline). Includes a 16-question FAQ comparing Pectra to Dencun and Fusaka, plus links to live adoption data from growthepie's Pectra tracker.
- [Is Ethereum deflationary?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/is-ethereum-deflationary): Live answer to whether ETH's supply is currently shrinking or growing. Pulls the current annualised issuance rate and 30-day net supply change directly from growthepie's ETH supply tracker (`/v1/eim/eth_supply.json`). Explains the two opposing forces — EIP-1559 base-fee burn (active since August 2021) vs Proof-of-Stake staking-reward issuance (since The Merge in September 2022, which cut new issuance ~87%) — and walks through the historical periods when ETH has been deflationary vs inflationary. Includes a 15-question FAQ covering EIP-1559 mechanics, why the burn exists, L2 contribution to burn, post-Fusaka effects, and how Ethereum's monetary policy compares to Bitcoin's.
- [What is the difference between a ZK rollup and an Optimistic rollup?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/zk-vs-optimistic-rollup): Direct architectural comparison of Ethereum's two main rollup types — ZK rollups (cryptographic validity proofs, fast L1 finality) vs Optimistic rollups (assume-valid, 7-day fraud-proof challenge window). Covers which chains use which architecture (Arbitrum/OP/Base = Optimistic; zkSync/Linea/Scroll/Starknet = ZK), security model differences, EVM compatibility, withdrawal time, and the convergence trend toward hybrid Optimistic+ZK. Knowledge-based with curated chain examples from growthepie's master catalogue.
- [What is the difference between an Ethereum L2 and a sidechain?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/l2-vs-sidechain): Resolves the common confusion (especially around Polygon PoS) about which chains are actually L2s. An L2 derives security from Ethereum by posting data or state proofs back to mainnet; a sidechain has its own validator set and only a bridge connecting it to Ethereum. Covers the test ("can users withdraw using only Ethereum data?"), why this matters (security guarantees, bridge risk), and where Validiums/Volitions sit. References L2BEAT classification conventions throughout.
- [Which Ethereum L2 has the most total value secured (TVS)?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/most-value-secured-ethereum-l2): Top 10 L2s ranked by current TVS (total value secured, often called TVL) — the dollar value of all assets bridged to or natively held on each chain. Includes each chain's share of the L2 ecosystem total and 30-day change. Data from growthepie's per-chain TVL endpoint. Recomputed daily.
- [Which Ethereum L2 is the most profitable?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/most-profitable-ethereum-l2): Top 10 L2s ranked by profit (revenue from user fees minus L1 settlement cost) over the last 30 days, with 90-day and all-time profit columns for context. Plus L2 ecosystem total profit. Data from growthepie's per-chain profit endpoint. Explains how Dencun and Fusaka widened L2 profit margins by lowering settlement costs.
- [How much does Ethereum mainnet earn from L2s?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/ethereum-mainnet-revenue-from-l2s): Total L1 settlement fees ("rent") paid by Ethereum L2s to mainnet — daily, 30-day, 90-day, and all-time totals plus top L2 contributor breakdown. Explains the EIP-1559 burn / validator-priority split, how Dencun's blobs and Fusaka's PeerDAS reshaped L2 settlement economics, and how this connects to L2 profit and ETH deflation.
- [Which Ethereum L2s are Stage 1 or Stage 2 rollups?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/ethereum-l2-maturity-stages): Maps every tracked Ethereum L2 to growthepie's 4-level maturity classification (Maturing / Developing / Emerging / Early phase), with explicit cross-reference to **three parallel frameworks**: L2BEAT's strict Stage 0/1/2 (canonical protocol decentralisation), the Ethereum Foundation's own "maturity" assessment published on ethereum.org/en/layer-2, and growthepie's broader 0–3 maturity scale. Explains how the three frameworks relate, why most L2s deliberately stay at Stage 0, and what the trust-model implications are for users at each level.
- [What is Data Availability (DA) and what are the differences between providers?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/what-is-data-availability): Explainer on what Data Availability means for Ethereum L2s and a provider-by-provider comparison of Ethereum DA (blobs since Dencun), Celestia, EigenDA, and Avail. Covers the security/cost/throughput trade-offs, how Data Availability Sampling works, which L2s use which DA, and the L2BEAT distinction between rollups (Ethereum DA) and Validiums (alt-DA).
- [What was The Merge and how did it change Ethereum?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/what-was-the-merge): Knowledge-based answer about Ethereum's transition from Proof-of-Work to Proof-of-Stake on September 15, 2022. Covers the three immediate effects (issuance dropped ~87%, energy consumption fell ~99.95%, conditions for ETH deflation were created), the seven-year journey from Casper FFG research to mainnet, and how The Merge enabled later upgrades (Dencun, Pectra, Fusaka). Live current-issuance-rate placeholder.
- [Which Ethereum L2 has the most smart-account (EIP-7702) activity?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/most-smart-account-activity-ethereum-l2): Ranks Ethereum L2s by EIP-7702 (Set Code / Type 4) transaction count — the live adoption signal for smart-account features introduced by the Pectra upgrade. Data from growthepie's Pectra tracker. Explains what EIP-7702 enables (gas sponsorship, paying gas in non-ETH tokens, transaction batching), the typical adoption patterns, and how L2 activity compares to L1.
- [Where does most DeFi happen — Ethereum mainnet or L2s?](https://www.growthepie.com/answers/defi-l1-vs-l2): Direct L1-vs-L2 comparison of DeFi transactions and gas fees across windows (7d, 30d, 90d, 1y, all-time). Includes per-L2 contributor breakdown for the 30-day window. Uses growthepie's blockspace overview to read both Ethereum L1's and the all-L2-aggregate's DeFi category data. Explains why DeFi migrated to L2s (fee economics, composability, network effects) and why L1 still leads in absolute TVL despite L2s leading in transactions.

## Research & Analysis — Quick Bites

Data-driven short articles on Ethereum ecosystem trends. Published by Matthias Seidl (@web3_data) and contributors. Licensed CC BY-NC 4.0. Recent and representative Quick Bites include:

- [Scaling Ethereum Mainnet to 10,000 TPS](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/ethereum-scaling): Data-driven projection of Ethereum's path to ~10,000 TPS on L1 and million-TPS potential with L2s. Includes historical TPS dataset (2015–2026) and projected roadmap scenarios. Published October 2025.
- [Pectra: The People's Upgrade](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/pectra-upgrade): Analysis of the Pectra hard fork and its measured impact on Ethereum network metrics.
- [Building the World Ledger](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/anniversary-report): growthepie one-year anniversary report. Overview of the Ethereum ecosystem's growth and the platform's coverage expansion. Published August 2025.
- [Celo: 1 Year as Ethereum Layer 2](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/celo-anniversary-report): One year after its migration to an Ethereum L2, Celo's position as a leading blockchain for real-world stablecoin payments. Published March 2026.
- [EigenDA and MegaETH Scalability](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/eigenda-megaeth): Analysis of EigenDA's blob throughput and MegaETH capacity metrics. Published March 2026.
- [ETH Supply Dynamics](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/eth-supply): Data analysis of Ethereum's supply mechanics post-merge including issuance, burn, and net supply changes.
- [Arbitrum TimeBoost](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/arbitrum-timeboost): Analysis of Arbitrum's TimeBoost sequencing upgrade and its effect on transaction ordering and fees.
- [Base Commerce](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/base-commerce): Metrics on Base's growth as a consumer application and commerce platform.
- [Arbitrum-Hyperliquid Bridge Activity](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/arbitrum-hyperliquid-bridge): Analysis of bridging flows between Arbitrum and Hyperliquid.
- [Linea Burn Mechanics](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/linea-burn): Data on Linea's fee burn mechanism and its economic impact.
- [Fusaka Protocol Upgrade](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/fusaka): Impact analysis of the Fusaka upgrade on Ethereum's capabilities.
- [Stablecoins by Chain](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/stablecoins-for-chain): Stablecoin supply distribution and trends across Ethereum chains.
- [Stablecoins by Project](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/stablecoins-for-project): Stablecoin supply breakdown by issuer (USDC, USDT, DAI, FRAX, and others).
- [Stablecoins by Fiat Currency](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/stablecoins-for-fiat): Stablecoin supply breakdown by fiat peg, token, and chain. Published April 2026.
- [EIP-8004: AI Agent Registrations](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/eip-8004): Data on AI agent registrations under ERC-8004, including agent ratings and service endpoint activity.
- [Octant V2 Migration](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/octant-v2-migration): Analysis of user and fund migration during the Octant V2 protocol transition.
- [Polygon Burn](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/polygon-burn): Data on POL/MATIC token burn activity and its effect on supply dynamics.
- [Robinhood Stock on Base](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites/robinhood-stock): Analysis of Robinhood's onchain stock trading activity on the Base network.

## Data Access

- [Public API](https://api.growthepie.com): Machine-readable JSON endpoints for all tracked metrics. Includes historical and real-time data for chains, fundamentals, and applications. No authentication required. Licensed CC BY-NC 4.0.
- [Documentation](https://docs.growthepie.com): Product and API documentation.
- [GitHub](https://github.com/growthepie): Open-source backend (gtp-backend) and frontend repositories. Data pipeline code, metric definitions, and contribution guidelines.

## Indexes

- Per-answer plaintext: append `/llms.txt` to any /answers/<slug> URL for a small, single-question markdown file (direct answer + full prose + FAQ). The preferred citation target for a specific question — see the Answers section above.
- [Full Content (Answers section)](https://www.growthepie.com/llms-full.txt): Single-fetch plaintext dump of every page in the /answers section — direct answer, full prose, and FAQ for each — recomputed daily with live data substituted from growthepie's API.
- [Main Sitemap](https://www.growthepie.com/sitemap.xml): Canonical index of public pages.
- [Chains Sitemap](https://www.growthepie.com/chains-sitemap.xml): Index of chain dashboard pages.
- [Fundamentals Sitemap](https://www.growthepie.com/fundamentals-sitemap.xml): Index of ecosystem-wide metrics pages.
- [Quick Bites Sitemap](https://www.growthepie.com/quick-bites-sitemap.xml): Index of Quick Bites pages.

## Optional

- [Donate](https://www.growthepie.com/donate): Support growthepie's open data mission.
- [Imprint](https://www.growthepie.com/imprint): Legal entity details — orbal GmbH, Möckernstr. 120, 10963 Berlin, Germany. Representative: Matthias Seidl. Register: Amtsgericht Charlottenburg HRB 252443 B.
- [Privacy Policy](https://www.growthepie.com/privacy-policy): GDPR-compliant data processing policy.
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/growthepie): Company profile and team updates.
- [Twitter / X](https://twitter.com/growthepie_eth): Latest data findings, chart threads, and platform updates.
- [Discord](https://discord.gg/fxjJFe7QyN): Community discussion and feedback.
